mappings.rationaleAI: Ptolemy's geocentric model in the Almagest used mathematical systems of epicycles and deferents to explain observed motions of the Sun, Moon, and planets from Earth, representing the foundational attempt to describe Earth's relationship to celestial bodies and the apparent revolution of the heavens—the historical precursor to understanding Earth's own revolution about the Sun (A.1).

This ancient astronomical computer calculated positions of the sun, moon, and known planets while tracking eclipse cycles, encoding the orbital relationships between Earth and other solar system bodies. Its modeling of celestial mechanics reflects early quantitative approaches to Earth's revolution and rotational periodicity (A.1).
